assertion.text: Clinical failures of anti-amyloid therapeutics reflect intervention timing and target-engagement issues rather than falsification of the upstream causal role of amyloid. Predictions: Anti-amyloid agents administered in preclinical or prodromal stages (e.g., DIAN-TU, A4 follow-ups) will show larger effect sizes than symptomatic-stage trials.; Trials achieving deeper plaque clearance on PET will correlate with greater cognitive benefit, even if absolute effect sizes remain modest.

evidence_spans.text: The note treats clinical failure and hypothesis validity as separable, implying the failures do not constitute disconfirming evidence of the cascade itself.

provenance.title: Amyloid cascade hypothesis remains dominant despite clinical failures.md
